vips.name: Ahmed Bazza Abaza
vips.title: CEO
vips.note: Built Gaby. Close friend. Call him Bazza.

suggested_intros.rationale: AAST is already a Sprintly partner for founder mentorship (Arab League). Sahat Venture Studio is a venture builder that could co-invest or refer founders to MENA to the Valley. Connecting Heba with Sohayb creates a pipeline bridge: AAST graduates -> Sahat validation -> Sprintly acceleration.
suggested_intros.draft_email:
Hi Heba, Hi Sohayb,

I wanted to connect you both. Heba runs the AAST Entrepreneurship Center in partnership with the Arab League, building some of the strongest early-stage founders in Egypt. Sohayb leads Sahat Venture Studio, working with ventures at the studio/validation stage.

I think there is a natural overlap here: AAST graduates who need venture validation support, and Sahat ventures that could benefit from AAST's ecosystem and mentorship network.

Would love for you both to explore how you might collaborate.

Warm regards,
Yousra Gaballah
Founder, Sprintly Partners

suggested_intros.rationale: Money Fellows is actively exploring US market entry (2 meetings on Yousra's calendar this week). Jonathan is a Draper University and BT150 alumni with deep Silicon Valley connections. As an AI CEO, he understands fintech infrastructure. This intro could help Money Fellows navigate the SV ecosystem for their US expansion.
suggested_intros.draft_email:
Hi Mazen, Hi Jonathan,

I wanted to introduce you both. Mazen is a Senior Product Manager at Money Fellows, one of Egypt's leading fintech platforms. They are actively exploring US market entry.

Jonathan is the CEO of Ironmind.ai and a Draper University and BT150 alumni with strong Silicon Valley roots.

Given Money Fellows' US expansion plans and Jonathan's experience building in the Valley, I thought this connection could be valuable for both of you.

Warm regards,
Yousra Gaballah
Founder, Sprintly Partners

suggested_intros.rationale: Two investor-side contacts who should know each other. B8 Business Angels (North Africa/Morocco) and Opel Ventures could co-invest in MENA startups coming through the Valley program. Cross-border angel/VC connections strengthen Sprintly's investor arm and deal flow.
suggested_intros.draft_email:
Hi Mehdi, Hi Tamer,

I wanted to connect two investors I respect in the MENA ecosystem. Mehdi is Vice President at B8 Business Angels, active in North Africa. Tamer leads Opel Ventures as President and CEO.

I believe there is strong potential for cross-border deal flow and co-investment between your organizations, particularly around MENA startups scaling globally.

I will let you both take it from here.

Warm regards,
Yousra Gaballah
Founder, Sprintly Partners

person_a.name: Shimaa El-Sheikh
person_a.title: Partnerships and Communication Lead, CREATIVA Hubs

suggested_intros.rationale: ITIDA is a Sprintly ecosystem partner. Shimaa leads partnerships at CREATIVA Hubs (Egypt's innovation hubs network). Hasan runs 'Bridging to Saudi,' which aligns perfectly with Sprintly's cross-border mission. Connecting Egypt's innovation infrastructure with a Saudi bridge operator creates a new referral channel for the MENA to the Valley program.
suggested_intros.draft_email:
Hi Shimaa, Hi Hasan,

I wanted to introduce you both. Shimaa leads Partnerships and Communication at ITIDA's CREATIVA Hubs, Egypt's innovation hub network supporting tech entrepreneurs. Hasan is the Founding Partner of Bridging to Saudi, facilitating cross-border business between markets.

Given the growing Egypt-Saudi startup corridor, I thought connecting CREATIVA's founder community with Hasan's Saudi bridge could create valuable opportunities for both ecosystems.

Warm regards,
Yousra Gaballah
Founder, Sprintly Partners
